reviews.title: Huge disappointment
reviews.text:
I am a big supporter of Polar products, my M400 was an absolute beast. I've since moved on to the Vantage V and it's by far the best training watch I've ever owned. This watch was a mess from the start...

I can't in good faith give this anything but 1 star, which it earned because of the HR monitor built in and I didn't seem to have any issues with tracking or accuracy, but that is pretty common faire these days and warrant just 1 star. I was just so damn tired of the HR strap that this was great at the time.

The power use or battery life (could never figure it out) was complete garbage. I understand that the target demo for this watch was never the ultra-marathon crowd, but this thing could barely make it through a day let alone a workout within that day. I disabled the "smart watch" features, enabled the smart watch features, ran the battery dead between wears, tried to always keep it charged, etc., And nothing seemed to matter. The battery would go from full to "not enough charge for workout" so erratically that it felt like keeping this thing working was the real workout. Mid-run, EVEN AFTER DISCONNECTING FROM THE PHONE, it would drain so quickly that it was always a gamble if it would make it through a 3 hr run (tops). Also, when this thing starts dying, you lose your tracking data first, then the HR, then it's a brick on your wrist. All within about 14-15% to zero, which was also pretty erratic. The sleep monitoring features are a wash because you have to charge this thing constantly so it only makes sense to do it at night.

The 2 button layout was a failure... Swiping is great (in theory) but the screen is pretty small, the gestures awkward, "swipe to nothing" screens and such made navigation miserable. (Possibly an Android Wear issue but no one needs to change their watchface swiping in 2-3 directions.) Secondly, this is a workout watch, you get sweaty (or drenched here in FL). This renders the watch's screen completely useless and I can't tell you how many corners I stood at pressing the screen trying to resume a run, change the metrics on my screen, etc. The standard workout screen also requires you swipe down for some stats so you just give up after a while. For normal daily use, I kept changing watch faces (which were very clunky to modify) or ending up at the settings menu. An Android Wear update resolved some of this but very iffy at best.

Once said run was done, syncing became an issue. I finally figured out that if you completely disconnected the watch, turned off any Bluetooth on the phone, did the workout, then slowly added the connections back, you had the best chance of the watch properly syncing with Flow and Strava. 60-70% of the time... If you forgot, all bets were off. I don't generally run with the phone and watch, but the few times I did it destroyed the battery life because it kept pinging the watch (also may be Android Wear), even when the link was severed/watch disconnected.

Onto the smart watch features. I won't beat a dead horse with the things noted above, but other than vibrating for calls or text messages, this was mostly useless. The slow response and connection time between the watch and my Pixel 2 had me constantly talking and repeating myself to my wrist like a lunatic. Then there are the 2-3 confirmations and need for a notary to send anything from the watch.... The mic idea is a good one and I suppose having the OK Google feature might be handy, but it mostly steers you back to your phone for anything useful, so I just ended up picking up the phone and bypassing the watch 90% of the time. This of course is not to mention the drain on the battery... I was really hopeful about the wrist nav and Google maps since I travel a bit but it just didn't pan out and I continued to rely on just the phone. They also sell that as a running feature (navigation) but you've got to pair with the phone so we're back to battery concerns.

The watch itself sits in a rubberized strap for removal/cleaning, which makes it large/bulky. Was difficult wearing under a dress shirt as a daily activity monitor. The Vantage V isn't small either, but it has a more classic watch look rather than time-traveler band so it blends better and I can wear under a cuff. If this watch isn't separated and rinsed after each use, it gets funky fast. Lotta nooks and crannies in there, be sure to clean it well or smell like a locker room. The band also doesn't breath at all so I would have to take days off wearing it because a rash would form. Wearing it loosely to help with this was uncomfortable due to its size so there wasn't really a win to be found there.

Polar really missed the mark on this one and I was really bummed that this was the direction they were taking their product line. Thankfully, right at the apex of frustrations, the Vantage V came out and they redeemed themselves. It's not a small price jump and I don't know too much about the Vantage M, but literally anything would be better than this watch and the V is by far the most impressive item they've delivered on.

reviews.title: Not Rating Watch
reviews.text: I’ve spent thousands of dollars on Polar watches, speedometers, etc, for my bikes and gym. I’ve had trouble off and on with charging cables. Although they have been great to replace charging cables, they outmode their products, so they become useless even tho they still work. I had a bad accident so was rehabbing for two years and had just started training in my gym and on bikes to compete again. I just got a notice in the mail, that the online training program will no longer be available I would have to buy a Polar Beat. I still need to know V2o, heart rate zones, training zones, so many other things that helped me train to my full potential. Even my Polar gym watches don’t work. I guess I can throw them out, although they still work. That being said, they make a good product, but so many become outmoded so you have to keep buying them.

reviews.title: So much potential but not worth the headache
reviews.text: I have had this for almost one year.  Very soon after I got it, it crashed.  I called customer service and reset it.  It took awhile and I was annoyed but the problem resolved.  Then again, similar issue.  This time the bluetooth and GPS stopped working.  I have used it for months with very limited capability - to track steps and heart rate - but decided to try to do another factory reset.  Lo and behold, that did not work so I guess I will be returning it to Polar.  I bought this because the heart rate monitor was supposed to be the best, and it is very accurate.  But it seems that Polar is not able to consistently deliver a quality product.

reviews.title: Very accurate on counting steps

Just keep in mind if you are out in public with pants that don't cover it, folks may look at you. It looks a little like an electronic monitoring bracelet. Which is fun to me. Let them wonder what I've been up to.....

reviews.text: It's nice for working out. But it does not track heart rate with a darn. Lights facing in though the mesh tight on the skin and nadda. A big disappointment. Directly on the skin, adjusted all around. Charge 3

reviews.title: Didn't count steps
reviews.text: I used this for a few weeks because I didn't feel my band was counting steps when I was shopping or carrying something.  I was very surprised to see that after a full day out-and-about I had under 300 steps on the counter.  That's roughly what I would normally have when I got up to get my morning coffee.  I tried re-adjusting and tried the other leg, but got the same result.  I'm back to keeping my tracker on my wrist.  Disappointed.

reviews.title: Comfortable and Lightweight
reviews.text: I love this! It is exactly what I need to help me keep moving and to accurately register steps as I work to increase my activity! The band is light and very comfortable. I hardly even notice that I have it on. The band does have some stretch to it which helps in making it easy to put on.

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.text: This band has helped me continue to get my steps when I am watching and carrying grandchildren, pushing a stroller or grocery cart, doing a lot of carrying around the house...I love it!  It is comfortable and secure!  With four snaps I can't imagine how you could lose it!

title: Kimnix Smart Watch 2022(Make Call Receive/Dial), 1.72 in HD Full Touch Screen Fitness Tracker with Bluetooth Call/Text/Heart Rate/SpO2/Sleep Monitor Smartwatch for Android iOS Mens Women (Black)

reviews.title: Fair features for the price
reviews.text:
First of all, you must keep in consideration that this is a $50 watch. That being said I will state what I consider are the pros and cons:

Pros
Price, battery life (more than the typical 1-day of apple watches), capable of answering and dialing within the watch (Bluetooth required) and blood oxygen and heart rate sensors.

Cons
Strap does not hold to watch and you must keep correcting it (solved with another strap?), blood pressure monitor? How???, you only have five watch faces at any given time but you can only replace one of the five - the others are permanently set, you get to edit the fourth one by adding a photo

Overall, there are cheaper and better watches like the WYZE (which I own and has better quality) but lack the phone answering and dialing features.

I will be replacing the strap with another type to see if it holds.

reviews.title: It does not keep all of the apps current. That is what I bought it for.

reviews.title: So glad I found this one!
reviews.text: I am so happy with the way this looks. It doesn’t look a traditional smart watch. It is more stylish. Would purchase again

reviews.title: Looks great but a pain to navigate
reviews.text: I ordered this watch because I love the style. I don’t care for the styles of most smart watches I’ve seen.  This watch looks great, very stylish!!  It comes with a tool to allow you to adjust the band length which is very helpful.  It keeps time perfectly.  It counts your steps and monitors sleep quality. The heart rate and BP monitors seem to be accurate as they are right on with my normal numbers. It measures sleep quality.  The hours I sleep is accurate but I don't know how to tell if the deep vs. light sleep is correct.  I assume its based on heartrate which seems to be accurate.  I cannot get the female app to sync, however,  I don’t use this feature. The apps are a pain to navigate through but aside from the female app they work well. The best part of this watch aside from its ascetics is that the charge lasts four to five days.

reviews.title: does what it's supposed to
reviews.text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price.
reviews.text: After buying the earlier brands of Fitbit which I loved. I bought myself a Luxe on sale for $100 last December 2021. It didn’t record anything accurately and just didn’t function the way it should. CS was if no help. I switched to Amazfit!  This is a great watch, the battery has lasted over a week. It accurately tells time, my heart rate, steps and I love this watch. It’s great and is so light on my wrist!!

reviews.title: Terrible smart watch, inaccurate and waste of time/money
reviews.text: After 5 months of use I’m done with this. It seems to be getting less accurate every workout. It can’t even get step counts correct. The accuracy of the heart beat monitoring varies too. The sleep monitor is often wrong about awake times so I don’t believe the other sleep data either. The only thing I can believe with this watch is the time of day.

reviews.title: Good budget watch if you don't need always-on display
reviews.text: Overall, this is a very reasonably priced watch with a lot of functionality. However, the fact that it doesn't have the always-on screen option was a deal breaker for me and I had to replace it with one of their other models.

reviews.title: Did not like this nearly as much as my original amazfit bip watch
reviews.text:
I bought an Amazfit Bip watch a year or more ago and have really been enjoying it The app tracks all my cycling trips and the sleep monitoring is useful. Iam really a big fan of the long battery life of the older model.

This watch has a 9 day battery life which is not nearly as long as the 30 days of the original watch.

This watch meausres blood oxygen levels but taking the measurement requires holding your wrist it the right position and takes much longer that the finger tip device Ihave.

I did not exhaustively evaluate the new app that came with this phone but it did not look nearly as good as the original app.

reviews.title: Really neat little smartwatch.
reviews.text: If you’re looking for a smartwatch that won’t break the bank but still provide handy applications at your literal fingertips, then this is a great buy! Works with iPhone or Android and it’s super easy to set up. The battery life so far is pretty good but I also haven’t had it for too long so will update if anything goes haywire. So far it’s exactly as described by the seller and it arrived quickly and packaged nicely. Great for self-use or a gift.

reviews.title: Not a Good Choice for Me
reviews.text:
Like - was able to call and receive texts. Was able to answer a call on the watch. Pairing was easy. The app was easy.
Dislike - Once paired, the watch takes all your media volume away from your phone. You won't hear any music like from YouTube, text message alert or phone ringing. The watch has a little vibration that you can barely feel, when you receive a text or getting a phone call. All sounds like from Facebook videos or anything, goes through the watch. The only way I could find to get it to quit doing this, was to unpair the watch totally. I need military time but couldn't find a way to make the time in a 24 hour format. You have to put the watch together. The bands come apart from the watch. Everytime you move your hand the watch face lights up. This was definitely not what I was looking for at all. Would I buy this again? No.

reviews.title: Not a good buy
reviews.text: The 1st one I got "went crazy" and started just cycling thru the various screens on the watch.  I returned it and got a replacement because we all know that "stuff happens".  I got the replacement and it seemed to be working well until day 28, when it stopped connecting with the app at all, even though I installed and uninstalled it several times as the troubleshooting recommended.  Then it started draining my battery dry and that meant it needed to be returned.  Don't waste your money on this one unless you have LOTS of time to fiddle with it!

reviews.title: I wanted to like this product...
reviews.text:
I wanted a simple activity tracker and this looked like it was perfect. There are a number of things that worked well, battery life, simplicity, matched my needs... However, the accuracy is non existent. I was at the gym working out vigorously and my heart rate was 50 and dropping. I checked it with another app on my phone and I was approaching 140. When I am at rest it creeps into the 90's. by the way, I have entered all profile info. The steps are also way off as I tested against an app on my phone.

I tried but its not worth the effort.

reviews.title: Awesome watch for the price!  On 2nd thought, maybe not so awesome
reviews.text:
I received this watch, opened it up and set it to charge for a couple of hours. I then followed the instructions to download the app onto my phone. I went to settings and pretty much turned the settings on for everything so that I could see if it would work. It does. I can check the weather, my temperature, blood pressure, oxygen, pulse and steps. I can set it to a sport activity and there are multiple ones. I was able to go to settings on the phone app and select 1 out of 152 different picture settings (and I can change it as much as I want to). It came with the 2 bands- pink and light brown or tan. I have only worn the pink band so far and it is soft. The charge on the battery lasted a week, which is nice because if you just remember to charge it for 2 hours once a week on the same day, that would be easy to remember. I have not really used the camera function but did somehow try it out as I twisted my wrist, and the phone camera took a picture. I have not used the music part of the watch either, but that is because I do not have a music option on my phone right now. The only issue that I had was connecting it to Google Fit so that I could attach that to the Walker Tracker App (which I use for competitions for # of steps). That is okay though because I can manually put the daily steps in. Overall, I would definitely recommend this watch. I have never had an Apple or Samsung Watch, but with this one, if it breaks, I won't be crying like I would with one of the expensive watches... and it works for what I want it for!

6/3/22- Update- this watch has been acting nuts the last 2 weeks. I was hoping it would work itself out, but it has not. The watch randomly buzzes and/or blinks. Sometimes it zeroes itself out. I am getting really frustrated. I have asked a question above to see if I can get help, so I will see what happens and let you know. I have only had it for maybe 6 weeks.

reviews.title: Doesn’t work correct
reviews.text: It doesn’t read while I’m asleep disconnects and doesn’t detect. And apparently I have hypothermia cause it constantly reads my temp at 95.7. Very sad and disappointed . Good battery life tho

reviews.text: I was skeptical because of the price but wow! I've had the expensive $200 watch before and this one works better. I can customize the face, the battery lasts days, the band does not bother me when I sleep like the other bulky watches, very easy set up. Amazing buy and I will be buying my children both one to monitor their sleep. Get it!!

reviews.title: Super great! As good or better than Apple Watch.
reviews.text: Great!

reviews.title: Warning: Do not trust medical readings!
reviews.text:
When I first opened this and changed the pink silicone strap to the rose gold steel strap, I thought it was pretty, albeit quite large for my tiny wrist. But I got this specifically for the health monitoring and wanted something that looked a little classy. Well, the only thing that reads correctly on my watch is the time.

I downloaded the app and it wants you to create an account and to allow access to everything personal on your phone. No thanks. I was not interested in receiving texts, calls, and alerts anyway. I just wanted to monitor my pulse, blood pressure, and steps. Everything else was supposed to be a bonus for me.

I had two separate doctor appointments yesterday and was alarmed when I compared the findings.
* Steps: When we discussed my new "smart"watch, the one doctor told me he's a runner and tries to get 10,000 steps daily even when he works. Apparently I walked about a third of that despite driving most of the day. GO ME!
* Temp: In the prior two days I wore this, my temperature has always read in the 95-96 degree range. Temp at both offices was NORMAL.
* O2 stats are probably ok, but that wasn't checked, at least that I paid attention to.
* BP: Here's a HUGE problem. It has consistently been reading at 128/76 +/- only 4 points on systolic or diastolic. But in both offices, my systolic was wrong by more than 30-35 points! I just came off BP medicine and needed to monitor it, so this was shocking, to say the least.
* Pulse: The other consistently incorrect reading. The watch has been reading between 90-110 at rest. This is alarmingly conceivable considering my health concern, which is why I went for one appointment to have it checked. However, after an EKG, my heart is fine and my pulse was actually around 70-80 at both offices after a little walking.
*Sleep: Since I have several issues with sleep, I looked forward to this info. Apparently I sleep while at my computer drinking coffee in the morning, as well as on part of my long drive home yesterday.

So not that it even matters at this point, but the location and weather would never "set" on the app or watch either, even though I specifically turned on my location just for your app. It just stayed blank with lines. I guess that's better than telling me it's snowing when it's really 70 degrees, considering your other reliable results.

Honestly, if you're going to market this as an activity tracker with health monitoring, then it should be able to give at least SOMEWHAT reliable readings. Mine weren't even close. I checked my temp, pulse, and BP manually the moment after it was taken in both offices to verify accuracy and it was so far off I thought I may be in jeopardy. So a big thank you for causing additional anxiety thinking my BP was only slightly high when it was way too high, and giving me tachycardia when it was only a bit elevated. Add my freezing temp and I should probably be close to death.

This is one of the most disturbing, nerve-wracking items I've ever gotten on Amazon. Or probably anywhere.

reviews.title: The watch does a lot
reviews.text:
UPDATE: I was to early in writing my review. What I have noticed today is if Watch Call is connected to Bluetooth, you cannot use “Hey Siri” on the phone. So I disconnected Watch Call from Bluetooth and I could use Siri. However, now I can’t get Watch Call to reconnect to
Bluetooth. I have powered the watch off and on and that hasn’t helped. If Watch Call has to be reset each and every time to connect that is a deal
breaker. I will reach out to the seller, but it’s more likely I will return.

It’s a little larger than what I wanted, but overall it’s a nice watch. EXCEPT, the manual is Awful. It leaves so much out where you have to spend so much time trying to figure things out.

reviews.title: Bluetooth
reviews.text: What this product doesn't state is that while the watch is connected to your phone it will not let you to connect to wireless earbuds, wireless speakers, or even hear you Dexcom alarm!! Dexcom warns diabetics of high and low blood sugar levels. It's extremely important to hear those alarms. I didn't reach out to the seller but while you can disconnect the phone to get earbuds to work that means all features of the phone doesn't work.

reviews.title: Get lot's of compliments
reviews.text: Love the watch but it's hard to keep it binded to my phone I have to keep binding it every time I leave out of my house

reviews.title: Excellent value for price
reviews.text: I would describe this watch as something for health purposes rather that physical training. I really like the heart rate, blood pressure, and sleep tracker. Pedometer is also pretty accurate. I wish there were more color schemes. Overall it’s a great dupe for an iWatch or Samsung watch for 1/10th of a price.
